items.headline: Albemarle (ALB) Loses 25.1% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why a Trend Reversal May be Around the Corner
items.snippet: ALB currently has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), in the top 20% of over 4,000 ranked stocks based on earnings estimate revision trends and EPS surprises — a potential indication of a near-term turnaround.

items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately amid slowing EV demand in China, an inventory glut and prospects of increased supply from mine restarts (CATL Jianxiawo, Mineral Resources B

items.headline: The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
items.snippet: Albemarle is the world's largest lithium producer. Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA jumped 148% year over year to $664 million as lithium prices rebounded from 2025 lows. Albemarle used the recovery to retire $1.3 billion of debt during the quarter.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Analysts' average price target is $214.65. Shares trade for less than 12 times forward earnings, offering an entry point for long-term investors. Dividend yields an above-average 1.15% with a payout ratio of 46%.
